scenario_examples.title: Patient request child-route return
scenario_examples.summary: Requests keeps the original request anchor through the intake child route and restores it on return.

steps.label: Origin anchor pinned
steps.detail: The patient pins the current request row before opening the child route.

steps.label: Child route entered
steps.returnLabel: Returning to Requests / Request Needs Attention
steps.detail: The same shell opens the intake child surface and preserves a return contract to the request row.

steps.label: Origin anchor restored
steps.detail: Exiting the child route restores the same request anchor rather than defaulting to the top of the list.

scenario_examples.title: Patient record recovery preserves the nearest safe anchor
scenario_examples.summary: When record-follow-up continuity degrades, the shell keeps the departing record anchor visible as a stub and restores the nearest safe summary anchor.

steps.label: Record anchor selected
steps.detail: The patient is reading the latest record entry.

steps.label: Anchor invalidated in place
steps.stubLabel: Record Latest is unavailable, so the shell preserves a stub and points to the nearest safe anchor.
steps.detail: Artifact drift freezes quiet return and keeps the selected record anchor visible as a stub.

steps.label: Recovery-only restore
steps.stubLabel: The exact anchor could not be restored, so the shell preserves a visible stub and re-arms only recovery-safe posture.
steps.detail: Refresh restores the nearest safe record summary while preserving the departing anchor stub and recovery posture.

scenario_examples.title: Workspace queue to case detail quiet return
scenario_examples.summary: The workspace keeps the active queue row as the authoritative return target while the case canvas takes focus.

steps.label: Queue row selected
steps.detail: The reviewer pins the current queue row before opening the case canvas.

steps.label: Case canvas takes focus
steps.returnLabel: Returning to Queue / Queue Active Case
steps.detail: The child surface opens without losing the source queue row or return contract.

steps.label: Quiet return restored
steps.detail: Returning to the queue restores the original queue row anchor instead of selecting a neighbour.

scenario_examples.title: Operations stale return preserves a visible watchpoint stub
scenario_examples.summary: A cleared anomaly cannot silently disappear; the board keeps a stub and degrades to read-only preserve.

steps.label: Watchpoint selected
steps.detail: The operator pins the current anomaly watchpoint.

steps.label: Investigation opened
steps.detail: The diagnostic route stays inside the same shell and preserves a return contract to the board.

steps.label: Read-only preserve with stub
steps.stubLabel: The exact anchor moved, so the shell keeps a stub and restores the nearest safe anchor.
steps.detail: When the watchpoint clears, the board returns in read-only posture with a visible stub instead of jumping to a sibling row.

scenario_examples.title: Governance diff replacement requires acknowledgement
scenario_examples.summary: Replacing the active diff anchor with an approval target requires an explicit acknowledgement before the new anchor becomes dominant.

steps.label: Diff anchor selected
steps.detail: The reviewer pins the active diff block as the continuity anchor.

steps.label: Replacement stub shown
steps.stubLabel: Governance Diff was replaced and requires acknowledgement before the new anchor becomes active.
steps.detail: The shell keeps the old diff anchor visible and announces the required replacement acknowledgement.

steps.label: Replacement acknowledged
steps.detail: The approval anchor becomes the active selected anchor only after explicit acknowledgement.
